titleStmt.title: Letter from the Major E. Reade to Mabel FitzGerald, 31 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: E. Reade

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from the governor or Dartmoor Prison, Major E. Reade, to Mabel FitzGerald (née Washington McConnell) (1884-1958), wife of politician and intellectual Desmond FitzGerald (1888-1947). The letter advises Mrs FitzGerald that a letter from her husband is on its way to her. It also outlines the prison's letter and visiting regime.Mabel was a former governess in London. She later vigorously canvassed her former acquaintances in London's liberal circles for support for Francis Sheehy Skeffington during his hunger strike in 1915. Both Mabel and Desmond would serve in the GPO garrison during Easter Week.

correspAction.placeName: 2 Loretto Villas, Bray, County Wicklow, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
H.M. Prison, Dartmoor 31 May 1916. Sir Madam Thom wD Fitzgerald I have to acknowledge the receipt of your letter of the 29th inst
and to inform you in reply that the above named
has written a letter to you which will no
doubt reach you in a day or two. You
are entitled to reply to it. During the
first year of imprisonment letters & visits
are allowed every four months.
I am
Your obedient Servant, E. Reade
Governor. Mrs Fitzgerald 2 Loretto Villas Bray Ireland
